mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.07% signals ultra-low rate environment
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.5%, or Rank IC over 60-day horizon falls below 0.02 across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings multiples downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 619105M and HOUST at 1421K units
regime_hint: demand-side contraction with synchronized weakness across consumption and investment
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression model over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 105 signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure due to margin compression concerns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 105.0 and remains there for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 105.583 is near threshold
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO < 105 for 2+ months, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust labor market momentum that increases consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors anticipate earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS grows > 0.2% month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 144120K and UNRATE at 6.0%
regime_hint: strong labor market expansion with synchronized employment and unemployment improvement
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 264.793
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate model over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annualized signals late-cycle or recessionary conditions that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce cyclical exposure.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 2.0% for 2+ consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 18794.426 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 2% for 2+ quarters, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1500K units signals strong residential investment demand and forward economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate broad-based economic acceleration.
regime_hint: housing-led expansion with strong residential investment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1500K do not exceed baseline returns by at least 1.5% annualized (t-test p > 0.05).
